query: What is the probability that flight AA123 from ATL to LAX will be delayed tomorrow morning?
expected_answer: Use predict_flight_delay with airline=AA, origin=ATL, destination=LAX to get the delay probability. The model uses CatBoost with Platt calibration.
contexts: The CatBoost model predicts binary flight delay (arrival >= 15 minutes late).
predict_flight_delay requires: airline, origin, destination, month, day, day_of_week, scheduled_departure, scheduled_arrival, distance, scheduled_time.
The model outputs delayed (bool), probability (float), confidence (str), threshold (float).

query: Which airport has the highest historical delay rate?
expected_answer: Use get_airport_delay_stats for the airports of interest. O'Hare (ORD) and SFO typically have the highest rates (~24-25%).
contexts: get_airport_delay_stats returns delay_rate, avg_delay_minutes, and total_flights for a given IATA code.
ORD (Chicago O'Hare) has high delays due to severe weather and traffic congestion.
SFO (San Francisco) experiences frequent fog-related delays.

query: Is Delta Airlines more reliable than Spirit Airlines?
expected_answer: Delta (DL) has a delay rate around 17%, while Spirit (NK) is around 26%. Use get_airline_delay_stats to confirm with latest data.
contexts: get_airline_delay_stats returns delay_rate, avg_delay_minutes, total_flights, and name.
Delta Air Lines is consistently among the top-performing US carriers for on-time performance.
Spirit Airlines operates with tight schedules, leading to higher delay rates.

query: What time of day has the fewest flight delays?
expected_answer: Early morning flights (5-8 AM, HHMM 500-800) have the lowest delay rates (~12%) because aircraft start fresh with no accumulated cascade delays.
contexts: Early morning flights (5-8 AM) have ~12% delay rate — aircraft start fresh.
Evening flights (after 6 PM) accumulate cascade delays throughout the day, reaching 28-35%.
The sched_dep_period feature categorizes departures: morning (5-11), afternoon (12-17), evening (18-22), night.

query: Will a flight from ORD to JFK on a Monday in July likely be delayed?
expected_answer: Use predict_flight_delay with origin=ORD, destination=JFK, month=7, day_of_week=1. ORD has ~25% historical delay rate and July is peak storm season.
contexts: O'Hare (ORD) has one of the highest delay rates (~25%) among major US airports.
July is peak summer thunderstorm season — delays peak in June-July.
ORD to JFK is a high-congestion route prone to NAS delays.

query: What factors does the model use to predict delays?
expected_answer: The CatBoost model uses airline, origin/destination airports, route, distance, scheduled times, day of week, month, historical delay rates (rolling 7/30-day), and congestion features.
contexts: BASE_FEATURE_COLS includes: AIRLINE, ORIGIN_AIRPORT, DESTINATION_AIRPORT, ROUTE, DISTANCE, SCHEDULED_TIME, sched_dep_hour, sched_dep_period, is_weekend, day_of_year, doy_sin, doy_cos, rolling target encoding features.
OPS_FEATURE_COLS adds historical operational signals: tail cascade delays, origin departure delay history, weather/system/late-aircraft rates.
TRAIN_PRIOR=0.18 fills missing historical features at inference time.

query: How accurate is the flight delay model?
expected_answer: The CatBoost model achieves ~72% AUC with precision >= 66% at the chosen threshold (~0.607) on the test set (months 11-12).
contexts: CatBoost binary classifier trained on months 1-8, validated on 9-10, tested on 11-12.
Threshold optimized for precision >= 66% on the validation set.
Platt calibration + prior-shift adjustment applied before thresholding.

query: Does Alaska Airlines have fewer delays than American Airlines?
expected_answer: Yes, Alaska Airlines (AS) typically has a delay rate around 15%, compared to American Airlines (AA) at ~20%. Use get_airline_delay_stats to confirm.
contexts: Alaska Airlines consistently ranks among the most on-time US carriers.
American Airlines delay rate is typically around 20%.
get_airline_delay_stats returns real statistics computed from the training dataset.

query: What is the delay rate for flights departing from LAX?
expected_answer: Use get_airport_delay_stats with airport_code=LAX. LAX historically has around a 19% delay rate.
contexts: Los Angeles (LAX) has approximately 19% delay rate with avg delay ~6 minutes.
LAX delays are often caused by high traffic volume and occasional marine fog.
get_airport_delay_stats returns delay_rate, avg_delay_minutes, total_flights, top_delay_hours.

query: How does the model handle flights where historical data is missing?
expected_answer: Missing historical features (rolling delay rates) are filled with TRAIN_PRIOR=0.18 (the training dataset prior delayed rate). This is the expected behavior for new routes or tail numbers.
contexts: TRAIN_PRIOR=0.18 fills all NaN numerical feature columns at inference time.
Rolling features like te_airline_w7 represent the historical 7-day delay rate for the airline — NaN when no history exists.
CatBoost handles missing categorical values via the MISSING_TOKEN sentinel.

query: Which months have the highest flight delay rates?
expected_answer: June and July have the highest delay rates due to summer thunderstorms. December and January also see elevated delays from winter weather.
contexts: Delays peak in June-July (summer thunderstorm season) and December-January (winter storms).
September and October have the lowest delay rates historically.
MONTH is a feature in the model — the doy_sin/doy_cos encoding captures seasonal patterns smoothly.

query: What does confidence: high mean in the model output?
expected_answer: Confidence 'high' means the predicted probability is more than 15 percentage points away from the decision threshold (0.607). This indicates a clear signal in either direction.
contexts: confidence='high' when |probability - threshold| > 0.15.
confidence='medium' when |probability - threshold| > 0.05.
confidence='low' when the probability is within 5 percentage points of the threshold — outcome is uncertain.

query: Is an evening flight from DFW to DEN likely to be delayed?
expected_answer: Use predict_flight_delay with origin=DFW, destination=DEN, scheduled_departure around 1800-2000. Evening flights accumulate cascade delays and have 28-35% delay rates.
contexts: Evening flights (after 6 PM) have 28-35% delay rates due to accumulated cascade delays.
DFW (Dallas/Fort Worth) has ~18% historical delay rate.
DEN (Denver) has ~17% historical delay rate; both are moderate-delay airports.

query: What is a tail cascade delay?
expected_answer: A tail cascade delay occurs when a specific aircraft (tail number) arrives late on an earlier flight and cannot depart on time for subsequent flights. The model tracks this via tail_dep_delay_mean and tail_delayed_rate features.
contexts: tail_dep_delay_mean_w3/w5/w10: rolling mean of departure delays for the tail number over 3/5/10 flights.
tail_delayed_rate_w5/w10: fraction of flights for this tail number that were delayed in the last 5/10 flights.
These OPS features capture aircraft-level cascade risk and improve model AUC.

query: How reliable is the weather delay information in the model?
expected_answer: The model uses historical weather delay rates (origin_weather_rate_w90/w180) as rolling features based on past data, not real-time weather. For actual weather forecasts, external sources are needed.
contexts: origin_weather_rate_w90/w180: fraction of flights from this origin with weather delays in the past 90/180 days.
These are historical signals, not real-time weather data.
For live weather impact, supplement with current weather forecasts from aviation weather services.

query: What is the difference between delay probability and delay prediction?
expected_answer: The model outputs a probability (0-1) of delay via Platt calibration + prior-shift adjustment. The binary prediction (delayed: true/false) is made by comparing the probability to the threshold (0.607). A flight is predicted delayed if probability >= threshold.
contexts: CatBoost raw probability → Platt calibration → prior-shift adjustment → final probability.
Best threshold (~0.607) is chosen to satisfy precision >= 66% on the validation set.
Prior-shift adjustment corrects for the difference between validation and test set delay rates.

query: How does the model handle weekends vs weekdays?
expected_answer: The model includes is_weekend (1 if Saturday or Sunday, 0 otherwise) and DAY_OF_WEEK as features. Weekend flights often have different delay patterns than weekday flights.
contexts: is_weekend=1 for DAY_OF_WEEK in {6, 7} (Saturday, Sunday).
DAY_OF_WEEK is also used in te_origin_dow_w30 — rolling delay rate by origin+day of week combination.
Weekends typically have lower business travel and different congestion patterns.

query: What does the drift monitoring endpoint do?
expected_answer: The /drift endpoint loads test_features_ref.parquet, compares training-set features (reference) vs test-set features (current) using Evidently DataDriftPreset, and reports the fraction of drifted features.
contexts: detect_and_log_drift reads data/processed/test_features_ref.parquet saved during make train.
Reference = months 1-8 (training split), Current = months 11-12 (test split).
Drift is reported as drift_share (0-1). warning_threshold=0.1, retrain_threshold=0.2.

query: Can the model predict delays for international flights?
expected_answer: No — the model was trained on US domestic flights (2015 dataset). International flights may involve different airports (not in the reference data) and will use MISSING_TOKEN for unknown codes, falling back to TRAIN_PRIOR for historical features.
contexts: Training data: US domestic flights from the 2015 flights.csv dataset.
Unknown airline/airport codes receive MISSING_TOKEN during sanitize_cat_cols.
Historical rolling features for unknown codes are filled with TRAIN_PRIOR=0.18.

query: How should I interpret a flight with confidence: low?
expected_answer: Confidence 'low' means the probability is very close to the decision threshold (within 5 percentage points). The model is uncertain — either outcome is plausible. Consider external factors like weather forecasts before making decisions.
contexts: confidence='low' when |probability - threshold| <= 0.05.
In this range, small changes in conditions can flip the prediction.
Low confidence predictions should be treated with caution; use additional information sources.
